title: hieracium glaucinum subsp. leucotephrodes
intro: Hieracium glaucinum subsp. leucotephrodes, also known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Hieracium glaucinum subsp. leucotephrodes include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce pain and inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. The plant is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
